'''PUBG Corporation''' is the subsidiary of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bluehole_(company) Bluehole Inc.] responsible for the development of ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PlayerUnknown%27s_Battlegrounds PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds]''. The studio, originally named Ginno Games, was acquired by Bluehole in March 2015 <ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.zdnet.co.kr/news/news_view.asp?artice_id=20150127114002 |title=블루홀스튜디오, '데빌리언' 지노게임즈 인수 추진 |publisher=ZDNet Korea}}</ref> and later renamed to ''PUBG Corporation'' in September 2017 following the success of the game. <ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/2017/09/29/playerunknowns-battlegrounds-studio-becomes-pubg-corporation/ |title=Playerunknown’s Battlegrounds studio becomes PUBG Corp. |date=2017-09-27 |publisher=RockPaperShotgun}}</ref>

'''Peacekeeper Elite''' (also known as '''Game For Peace (GFP)''') is an online multiplayer mobile battle royale game developed by [[Lightspeed & Quantum Studios]] and published by [[Tencent Games]]. It was released on May 8th, 2019 and is the official Chinese version of [[PUBG Mobile]].

===Format===
* '''OneGame Pro Championship Season 1''' : ''March 29th - April 17th
** 32 teams.
** Squad, {{Abbr/TPP}}.
* '''Quarter Finals''' : ''March 29th - 31st
** 32 teams.
*** 26 invted teams.
*** 6 teams from Qualifiers.
** 3 matchdays.
** Teams are divided equally into 4 groups of 8 teams.
** Round-robin format.
** {{bgcolortext|up|Top 24 teams}} qualify for ''Semi Finals.
* '''Semi Finals''' : ''April 2nd - 3rd
** 24 teams.
** 2 matchdays.
** Teams are divided equally into 3 groups of 8 teams.
** Round-robin format.
** {{bgcolortext|up|Top 16 teams}} qualify for the ''Grand Finals.
* '''Grand Finals''' : ''April 8th - 10th, 15th - 17th
** 16 teams.
** 6 matchdays.

S3 |shortname=Season 3 |image=OneGame Pro Championship Season 3 lightmode.png |imagedark=OneGame Pro Championship Season 3 darkmode.png |icon=OneGame Pro Championship Season 3 lightmode.png |icondarkmode=OneGame Pro Championship Season 3 darkmode.png |type=Online |team_number=16 |series=OneGame Series |organizer=OneGame Esports |organizer-link=https://www.instagram.com/onegameesports |organizer2=One Digital Entertainment|organizer2-link=https://www.onedigitalentertainment.com/ |mode=squads|perspective=tpp |localcurrency=inr|prizepool=500000 |country=India |sdate=2025-12-20 |edate=2025-12-22 |rulebook= |twitter= |facebook=profile.php?id=61555969237873 |instagram=onegameesports |youtube=@One.GameOfficial |web=https://one.game/ |discord= |previous=OneGame/Pro_Championship/Season_2{{!}}Season 2 }} ==About== ===Format=== * '''Pre Quarter Finals:''''' December 12 - 15, 2025 ** 64 teams, 4 matchdays. *** 32 qualified teams. *** 32 invited teams. *** Each group will play 6 matches. ** Teams are divided equally into 4 groups of 16. ** {{Bgcolortext|up|Top 8 teams from each group (Total 32 teams)}} advance to Quarter Finals. ** {{Bgcolortext|down|Bottom 8 teams from each group}} are eliminated.
* '''Quarter Finals:''''' December 16 - 17, 2025 ** 32 teams, 2 matchdays. ** Teams are divided equally into 2 groups of 16. ** Each group will play 6 matches. ** {{Bgcolortext|up|Top 4 teams from each group (Total 8 teams)}} advance to Grand Finals. ** {{Bgcolortext|stayup|Bottom 12 teams from each group (Total 24 teams)}} advance to Semi Finals.
* '''Semi Finals:''''' December 18 - 19, 2025 ** 24 teams, 2 matchdays. ** Teams are divided equally into 3 groups of 8. ** Round-robin format. ** Each group will play 8 matches. ** {{Bgcolortext|up|Top 8 teams}} advance to the Grand Finals. ** {{Bgcolortext|down|The rest}} are eliminated.
* '''Grand Finals:''''' December 20 - 22, 2025 ** 16 teams, 3 matchdays. ** 18 matches (6 each day). {{box|start|padding=1em}} ====Points Distribution==== {{Points10|toggle=false|killterm=finish}}
